title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in evaluation and security rather than policy: new work proposes benchmarks for bio-capability risk and world-model reliability, alongside a privacy-focused approach to confidential LLM serving. NIST also adds a notable systems-level governance argument for continuous monitoring, reinforcing a shift from one-time assurance toward ongoing oversight of AI deployments.

items.title: NIST backs continuous monitoring and updating for AI security
items.why_it_matters: It strengthens the case for ongoing assurance over one-time certification of AI systems.

items.title: ABC-Bench proposes an agentic bio-capabilities benchmark for biosecurity
items.why_it_matters: It targets a high-consequence risk area where better evaluations could inform deployment safeguards.

items.title: OpenPCC explores confidential LLM serving on commodity TEEs
items.why_it_matters: It addresses privacy and cloud trust by reducing provider access to model inputs and outputs.

items.title: WorldOlympiad introduces a benchmark for physical reliability in video world models
items.why_it_matters: It evaluates whether world models are faithful enough for safety-critical planning and agent use.

items.title: Predicting future behaviors in reasoning models improves steering
items.why_it_matters: It suggests a more anticipatory way to control model behavior with less quality degradation.

items.title: Multi-faceted interactivity alignment targets full-duplex speech model behavior
items.why_it_matters: It focuses alignment on conversational behavior, not just token prediction, for more controllable voice agents.

items.title: Flaws in the LLM automation narrative questions benchmark-based capability claims
items.why_it_matters: It challenges evaluation practices that can overstate reliability on real-world expert tasks.

items.title: Data Journalist Agent emphasizes verifiable multimodal story generation
items.why_it_matters: It centers verification in agentic content generation, relevant to provenance and misinformation risks.

items.title: Study examines governance strategies for insecure SOHO device supply chains
items.why_it_matters: It highlights governance levers for persistent security failures in widely deployed connected devices.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
